item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Always loved this one
+1

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Licensing, Open
item.content:encoded:
Sorry about the wait, yall!

new image attribution
– hide block

Reddit image is CC by 2.0

Source

Tumblr image is also CC.

Source

Wojak image was drawn by daviiid using this as a reference. Used with permission.

The redacted image is my own work.

Bug tree image is CC by 2.0

Source

Reddit code was made by Lt Flops (I think). 4chan code was created by Nagiros. Tumblr code is modified Oneiroi goi format code.

item.title: Staff Post- Licensing, Open
item.content:encoded:
This article has been up for about two years and hasn't had its images sourced. Authors, please source your images within the allotted time or they will have to be removed. Some may have to be removed anyway because they are screenshots of websites with all rights reserved formatting.

Edit- the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 especially relevant rn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NOOOOOOOO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"if you have to go outside for any reason, cover your mouth. wear a scarf, pull up your shirt, just keep it covered"

Dude, like… coronavirus, masks…

… How did you know?

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This creeped me the fuck out, its honestly too realistic how each site reacts, and that makes it more terrifying.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Tumblr post saying "I don't want to die"

Huh

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this feels like the start of something promising, but screencaps alone aren't lending the substance that this apocalypse deserves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I can not breathe" -BTS

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this story but I'm confused by the World Health Ministry.

Is it an organization separate from the WHO? Or is it just this universe's equivalent?

Is the phrase "If you ever breathe outside" intended to sound awkward?
Like it was translated from a different language?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how the title makes it look like a 4chan green text post. How could I not upvote?

Creative idea and hilarious out of context. Good work author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Loved the ambiguity of what really went down. It was short and didn't spell out everything, I'm still wondering what was actually happening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd imagine there's something more going on than simply a weird narrative through social media.

I would guess that this is an alternate universe or this is a huge cover up for something that really happened. Maybe even time travel that left remnants of the alternate reality online?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
>alternate reality version

well,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I imagine its harmful because:
One: It reveals the foundation and infected thousands of computers, meaning many innocent and influential people had to be amnesticised, including some world leaders and celebrities, as well as the possibility of family members of personnel, which could convince some foundation members the organization their working for is run by dirt bags.
Two: It shows that an alternate reality version of the O5 council would willingly let a XK class end of the world scenario take place without intervention, as well as with the majority of foundation staff participating, meaning that either they had a better reason to let the world be destroyed than intervene, or that the council houses darker intentions than thought, and if any of that reality is the same as ours, there's no way the O5 would want anyone getting an easy glimpse at their agenda.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assure you the 4chan posts would have been far more accurate if my moral code let me say slurs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay I'm not sure what I'm looking at here. It's not even a harmful skip so why is it under O5 lock and key? Nothing here warrants this level of secrecy. I mean I like the article itself but I don't understand whats the big deal and why its being presented like this. Can someone explain? No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
>be me
>necrophiliac, still alive
later virgins

That alone earned you a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how this can go in any way, ether this is a real "insect infection" or there posting from another world or maybe mass hysteria.

The format of posts from other site work great , I can't imagine the hard work toy photoshop all that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is one of the more bizarre of the newer SCPs. Gave me heebie jeebies, but that means you did good!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Decent article, a little confusing but nothing patience and focus cant fix. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Outstanding. The way you captured the "personality" of each site is perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi I’m new so maybe my opinion can be discarded as a wide-eyes newbie but I thought the stubble narrative (I’m gunna go with narrative for want of a better word ) shown through the posts on the affected sites was pretty good and I liked the small little things like how the user with the name written in a foreign language had the most likes. Also the title did its job and drew me in.
Upvote
Bug time

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. The posts were fairly realistic and I could feel the collective dread of the entire world succumbing to insects. I do wonder, however, on whether the alternate Foundation disappearing and the tweets appearing in our dimension are two connected events…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of Insect Apocalypse was a secondhand idea to the main one, which was just “Tell an apocalypse story through social media posts.” Insect Swarm of Death was a nice vehicle for that because insects are strange and can open up a variety of different apocalyptic pathways.

That’s all I really have to say since I mostly agree with your points. I’m not entirely sure where we thought the Middle East collapsing into the sea would connect to the rest

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn’t bad but it’s not good. It’s very much… a concept that doesn’t seem to expand very far. Firstly, your actual anomaly seems very disconnected from the story, that being insect infection apocalypse thing. It’s a very thin excuse to tell the story the way you want to — I can see the authorial thought process behind it too clearly, the illusion of natural storytelling is nil.

Then, the actual content feels underdeveloped. I think the appeal is the “memeiness”? I see that you substituted 4Chan for Facebook because it had more shitposty vibes to it, but that feel doesn’t land for me. And I think you could’ve done “shitposting in the face of apocalypse” — which I do think people would do if we were told the world would end tomorrow, heh — without the contrived setup of “apocalypse but we only saw it for 48 hours through the lens of social media for no apparent story relevant reason”.

The apocalypse itself is bugs that take you over and make you murder. It’s likely a bit more complicated than that, but that’s not important. It’s an apocalypse with a theme, you’re trying to deliver on how people react. Which you do, fairly realistically. But. The article doesn’t end on any special note. The “ignorance will be the death of humanity” post is not significantly more or less impactful than the rest of the article. Which is to say, the chord you are hitting is fine, but it’s the same chord. Throughout the article. Songs don’t get made with just one chord. So your ending feels weak, which is arguably one of the most important parts about your skip. You need to leave your reader on an interesting, different note than the rest of the article.

In all honesty though, not a bad article. I’m not offended by it being on the site, and I’m novoting. But I’m very mystified by it being at +485. I don’t feel that level of polish here.

Except in the creation of those screenshots, they look very real and good, so congrats on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's at +483 and has been up for ten months. Your comment is the third to mention Trump, the first being me nitpicking Trump's tweet location and the second saying the tweet sounds like Trump.

Why do the authors need good luck? There hasn't even been a hint of controversy regarding Trump and this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, all I can say is, good luck with this. The SCP Foundation Wiki is a tough room when it comes to articles involving Trump. I found that out the hard way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, I like it. As someone who's vaguely familiar with the cultures of all 4 of these sites you really did a good job of capturing the atmosphere of them, Tumblr in particular. The middle post in the 4chan section took me out of it a little bit, but I've never used 4chan and it's the site here whose culture I'm the least familiar with, so take that with a grain of salt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know I really do love this entry. I found it to be very creative. The mysterious nature of the event is really captivating. My interpretation is that our internet somehow slipped into an alternate reality for the time of the event. I also like how it's just that, an event. I've not come close to reading all the SCPs, but from the couple hundred I've read, this is a really unique concept. The visuals from each website really tie it all together, I think if it had just been a description of what each website had experienced it wouldn't have been as effective. Overall I gotta say, nice work. Get's a + from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not what I was expecting to see in the V series when I was browsing but I was pleased nonetheless. A bit short, but it gave me a good chuckle for my own strange reasons. The title was eye-catching to say the least!
Roughly 400 people decided "eh, this is okay i guess." Thank you all!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great SCP. Excellent example of an otherwise mundane concept executed brilliantly. I like how layered it is. At first, it seems as though it is a simple electronic anomaly, but it soon becomes apparent that there is more going on. I love the mystery. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This confirms what I think - the world will not end with a bang, nor a whimper, but with a 4chan post about how the apocalypse is 'unbelievably gay'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A shitpost to end all shitposts.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Channel 58! And yeah, this seems to have a similar concept to that.

item.title: "Dude, those aren't birds"
item.content:encoded:
Honestly that reddit conversation is just too real for how they'd act in an apocalypse.

+1 for that alone. ;P

item.title: Even in the apocalypse 4chan is still 4chan
item.content:encoded: Also i feel like it would have been weird if you went online one day and you saw yourself fighting for your life against a massive swarm of bugs

item.title: Re: +300
item.content:encoded:
OH YEAH
WE IN THERE BOIS
WE IN THERE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The second to last 4chan post got me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think my favourite part of this is how real it feels it terms of social media.

Fourchan with the lack of empathy and the 'for the lulz' attitude.

Tumblr with the mix of PSAs and strange humor.

Reddit's mumbo-jumbo political discourse.

Hell, you even had Trump on Twitter protrayed like well, Trump.

'숨을 못 쉬겠어' I can't breath.

That line made upvote.

Fantastic job.

item.title: +1'ed
item.content:encoded:
This has my upvote mostly because of the idea, but also the way it is written does its work.

The idea that an anomalous global shitpost could happen and make people all around the world ask "What is this SCP Foundation?" it's quite creepy in its own way.
Researchers and O5s in universe probably were about to nuke everything, but that's another issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I think it was plenty interesting. I wouldn't have wanted much more than what we got (maybe a couple more paragraph-long infodumps would have been okay, but preferably through one of the other social media sites, maybe even a conversation) but what we got worked perfectly for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this mainly due to how real it looked

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Happy April Fools Day!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even as the world ends 4chan shitposting still remains.

Nice

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: The bread has been got, gamers

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded:
epic

also 69th post heheheh
look at how my boy has grown

item.title: Re: Amazed.
item.content:encoded:
This is a separate thing from the main insect hell. It was just a coincidentally related doomsday scenario, and i ended up asking JackalRelated if i could include it in their hub.

So it fits in their canon, but in an entirely different universe. Maybe a warning sign for them observing it.

item.title: Amazed.
item.content:encoded:
After reading all the “insect hell” tales and this, it only leaves the imagination to determine what really happened. well done.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"what the fuck are you doing outside anon?"

legendary. The insects arent that interesting but the Broken Masquerade implication and social media stuff is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome concept and I love the fake social media reactions, you strangely captured the "personality" of each of these social medias perfectly, if that makes any sense. Reminds me of that fake "DO NOT LOOK AT THE MOON" news broadcast on youtube in some way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm saying realism shouldn't be compromised to "avoid exposition".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're very obviously missing the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Making coherent sense generally isn't seen as a failure.
